$comment: Shown one at a time on the install screen, crossfading every 8 seconds. Edit freely - this file is read at runtime and needs no rebuild. Mix genuinely useful with genuinely funny; a run is fifteen minutes and nobody wants forty facts in a row.
tips: Left Shift starts a traffic stop. Left Shift also starts most conversations you'll regret.
Numpad binds need Num Lock on. This will waste ten minutes of your life at least once.
Deadly Weapons means you die like everyone else. Stay in cover.
The vapid police cruiser is the only vanilla car that's ELS compatible - and only with both extras turned off.
If Script Hook V doesn't match your game build, nothing loads. Nothing. It's always this.
F4 opens the RagePluginHook console. It is not a bind you get to reassign, and yes, people try.
Callout Interface ships its own RageNativeUI. So does LIAR. Only one of them can win, and Dispatch already picked.
Steam's 'verify integrity of game files' deletes every mod you own and calls it a repair.
A pursuit you can't end is usually a pursuit you started at 4am on the freeway.
Grammar Police lets you talk to dispatch. Your housemates will have opinions about this.
Stop The Ped's pat-down is on F9 by default, which is also where four other mods want to live.
Backup takes a moment to arrive. Los Santos is bigger than it looks on the minimap.
The MDT will happily tell you a suspect has no record. The suspect will happily prove otherwise.
ELS lighting is configured per-vehicle. If your lightbar does nothing, check els.ini before rebuilding anything.
Antivirus will flag ScriptHookV.dll. It's an injection library. That's the entire job description.
Rockstar patches the game, Script Hook V rebuilds, and every forum post that day says LSPDFR is broken. It isn't.
K9 units are excellent at finding contraband and terrible at using doors.
Your dash cam records the department name you typed. Choose something you'll enjoy reading at 2am.
If a mod loads fine alone and breaks in a group, it's load order. It's almost always load order.
Traffic stops on the freeway: park behind and to the left. The game's traffic AI has not read the manual.
Radio Realism replaces eighteen scanner files. If dispatch goes silent, that's where to look first.
OpenIV edits go into the mods folder, never the game folder. Doing it the other way is how people lose an afternoon.
Spotlight is genuinely useful at night and genuinely hilarious pointed at a helicopter.
Most 'random crashes' are one plugin. Safe mode finds it in about ninety seconds.
Callouts don't spawn while you're stationary in an alley. Drive around; the city needs to see you.
Ultimate Backup and Search Items Reborn both replace Stop The Ped files. That's why they install last.
The Los Santos road network is roughly a fifth the size of the real LA basin, and still takes ten minutes to cross.
Arresting someone doesn't end the callout. Transport does. This surprises everyone once.
Left Control plus O opens the radio. Left Control plus most things opens something.
A plugin that needs .NET will fail silently without it. That's what the dependency check is for.
Charges and Citations has a twenty-four hour court wait by default. It is not broken; time just passes.
GTA V shipped in 2013. It has now outlived several consoles and at least one modding framework.
The police scanner audio is stitched from clips. If a street name sounds odd, that's why.
Heli Assistance needs its air unit callsign set or it will call you nothing at all.
Path traversal in a mod archive is an instant reject. There is no version of that which is fine.
If two mods want the same key, the one that loads later usually wins. Usually.
Simple Trainer's spawn-a-driver key defaults to something you will press by accident. Dispatch clears it.
Pulling someone over for a broken tail light and finding a felony warrant is the whole appeal, really.
The game's own police AI still exists underneath all this. Occasionally it has opinions.
Bait Car is on F11. Your patience is on a shorter fuse than you think.
Every mod here comes from its author's own server. Nothing is rehosted, which is why this takes a few minutes.
Config files keep comments through a Dispatch edit. Nothing is regenerated from a template, ever.
If this run fails halfway, the journal knows exactly where. Resume picks up from there.
Immersive Effects on F2 makes crashes hurt. Some people call that realism.
The fastest way to break a working setup is to install one more thing at 1am. Ask anyone.
Postal codes exist because 'somewhere on the west side' is not a location dispatch can work with.
Fifteen minutes of installing beats four hours of dragging files into folders. That's the whole pitch.
